<question>
What causes Parkinson's Disease?
A. Genetics
B. Being exposed to toxins
C. Head injuries
D. Brain infections
</question>
completion:
Parkinson's disease occurs when nerve cells, or neurons, in an area of the brain that controls movement die or become impaired. Normally, these neurons produce an important brain chemical known as dopamine, but once the neurons become impaired, they produce less dopamine and eventually none at all. Without dopamine, it becomes difficult for the brain to coordinate movement and produce smooth, normal movement.
<answer>A</answer>
